codeReview.summary: The feature correctly adds a compatibility-only `client.completions()` surface, preserved non-streamed `text_completion` parsing, and local `best_of` streaming validation, but the new stream accumulator fabricates an empty completion when a stream yields only `[DONE]` and no completion object.

issues.description: `CompletionAccumulator::finish()` accepts any stream that saw `[DONE]` or a terminal chunk, even if no completion payload was ever parsed. In the `[DONE]`-only case it returns a synthetic `Completion` with empty `id`, `created=0`, and no choices because required fields are filled with `unwrap_or_default()` at lines 343-348. That violates the legacy contract that streamed and non-streamed responses share the `text_completion` shape and masks malformed/truncated streams instead of surfacing a parse error.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared API-surface library notes that Legacy Completions exists, but it does not record the two key compatibility invariants this worker had to rediscover from references: streamed and non-streamed payloads share the same `text_completion` shape, and `best_of` cannot be streamed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/api-surface.md:17-19` only lists `Legacy Completions`. The worker transcript skeleton for session `16783e21-35c0-4b4f-8fc0-e1a8f7433df1` shows extra reference lookups (`Grep` for `text_completion|/completions|best_of` and `Read` of `references/openai-node/src/resources/completions.ts`) to recover those invariants.

summary: Fail: the legacy completions surface is wired correctly overall, but streamed parsing currently accepts a `[DONE]`-only transcript and returns a fabricated empty completion instead of an error.
